subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **108 double-sided cards** — One side shows a vegetable, the other shows a scoring condition.
- **6 vegetable types**: Pepper, Lettuce, Carrot, Cabbage, Onion, Tomato (18 each)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Remove cards** based on player count (use 6 vegetables per player).
2. **Shuffle remaining cards** scoring-side up.
3. **Deal into 3 draw piles** roughly equal.
4. **Flip 2 cards** from each pile to create a 3×2 vegetable market (6 face-up veggie cards).
5. **Scoring cards** remain on top of each pile, visible.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **No starting cards** — Players draft from the market.
- **Determine first player** — Most recently ate a salad (or random).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Point Salad is a fast card-drafting game. Collect vegetables and scoring cards to build the best-scoring combination. Each scoring card has a unique condition (e.g., "2 pts per Carrot" or "5 pts per Lettuce/Tomato pair").

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE of:
1. **Take 1 scoring card** from the top of any draw pile.
2. **Take 1 or 2 vegetable cards** from the face-up market.

Then refill the market from the corresponding draw pile(s).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Drafting Scoring Cards
- Take the top card from any of the 3 draw piles (scoring side up).
- Place it in your collection scoring-side up.

#### Drafting Vegetables
- Take 1 or 2 face-up vegetable cards from the market.
- Cards are replaced from their respective piles.

#### Flipping Scoring Cards
- On your turn, you may also flip one of your scoring cards to its vegetable side.
- This is permanent — you cannot flip it back.
- Useful if a scoring card doesn't fit your strategy but the vegetable does.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Empty pile** — If a draw pile runs out, redistribute from the largest pile.
- **Market empty** — If market slots can't be refilled, they stay empty.
- **Scoring variety** — Conditions include per-type bonuses, set bonuses, majority bonuses, and even penalties.
- **Negative points** — Some scoring cards give negative points for certain vegetables.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- When all cards are drafted (draw piles and market are empty).

#### Scoring
- Evaluate each scoring card against your vegetable collection.
- Sum all scoring card results.
- **Highest total wins.** Ties: most vegetable cards.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Synergy** — Pick scoring cards that work well with the same vegetables.
- **Don't over-diversify** — Focus on 2-3 vegetable types rather than all 6.
- **Flip bad cards** — If a scoring card hurts you, flip it to its vegetable side.
- **Watch opponents** — Don't compete for the same vegetables as your neighbors.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Stacking bonuses** — Multiple scoring cards rewarding the same vegetable compound quickly.
- **Deny drafting** — Take vegetables opponents need for their scoring cards.
- **Majority cards** — "Most X = 10 pts" cards are powerful but contested.
- **Timing** — Take scoring cards early to plan, then focus on vegetables later.
